---
title: "Lawrence Wu"
image: images/lawrence.jpg
comments: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 10em
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/lawrencewu920/
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/lawwu
      target: _blank
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/law_wu
      target: _blank
    - icon: youtube
      text: YouTube
      href: https://www.youtube.com/@lawrence_wu
      target: _blank
---

## About Me

I am a Data Scientist & AI Engineer developing Foundation Models and Agentic ML at UKG. I have 10+ years of experiencing building machine learning systems. In recent years I have developed expertise in LLMs, Agentic Harnesses and adapting to new ways of working with coding agents like Claude Code and Codex. I'm a lifelong learner and enjoy sharing what I've learned with others through writing. I believe writing is thinking.

## Subscribe {#subscribe}

You can subscribe to my blog via [email newsletter](https://lawrencewu.substack.com/) or using the [RSS feed](https://lawrencewu.net/index.xml).

I try to send out a weekly newsletter with things I've written on this blog as well as other things I've been reading:

```{=html}
<iframe src="https://lawrencewu.substack.com/embed" width="640" height="320" style="border:1px solid #EEE; background:white;" frameborder="0" scrolling="no"></iframe>
```

## Let's Connect

- [@law_wu](https://x.com/law_wu) on X/Twitter
- [Youtube](https://www.youtube.com/@lawrence_wu)
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lawrencewu920/)

I'm also open to consulting opportunities and collaborations. Check out [Doxa Solutions](https://doxasolutions.ai){target="_blank"} or reach out via any of the links above.

## About This Site

This site is made with [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) and [Jupyter](https://jupyter.org/) (for posts that are backed with code). The website is deployed via Github Actions to [Cloudflare Pages](https://pages.cloudflare.com/). You can find the source code at [lawwu/lawrencewu-blog](https://github.com/lawwu/lawrencewu-blog).

I also use Claude Code to help manage and administer the site. The writing is my own, except for some of the AI-generated summaries of talks.
